sections.paragraph:
ἐνστερνίζομαι pf. mid. ptc. ἐνεστερνισμένος (late word, only in Christian wr., usu. in mid.; Hesych.; Suda; Psellus p. 72, 17; B-D-F §123, 2) to store away within oneself ἐπιμελῶς ἐ. ἦτε τοῖς σπλάγχνοις (sc. τ. λόγους) you had carefully stored away in the depths of your being 1 Cl 2:1.—DELG s.v. στέρνον.

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. wr. = writers, writings

usu. usu. = usually

Hesych Hesych , V a.d.—List 5

Suda Suda = a lexical work (freq. cited as Suidas in handbooks); X a.d.—List 5

Psellus Psellus , History, XI a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 339
